source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from 50 kg-class microsatellite primary structure practice (RAPIS-1, Hodoyoshi, Innovative Satellite Technology Demonstration series): four corner rails, six wall panels, top/bottom deck plates, internal midplate for payload tray, launch-vehicle adapter ring, separation switch bracket, and M4/M5 fasteners. Load magnitudes, material properties, and pass/fail thresholds inherit verbatim from the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design the primary structure of a 50 kg class microsatellite for a student-demonstration microsatellite slot. The envelope is the published 50 kg class volume for the microsatellite program (approximately 500 mm x 500 mm x 500 mm with attachment ring); the total wet mass must not exceed 50 kg. The launch environment is defined per the program launch vehicle: random vibration 14.1 Grms (NASA GEVS qualification-style spectrum, 20-2000 Hz), and shock response spectrum 1000 G peak in the 1000-10000 Hz band at the separation plane. A quasi-static limit load of 20 g axial and 10 g lateral is applied at the spacecraft CG. The primary structure must carry and survive all three environments: quasi-static, random vibration, and pyroshock. Natural-frequency requirement: the first longitudinal mode must be above 110 Hz and the first lateral mode must be above 45 Hz, consistent with the launch vehicle decoupling requirement. Stress allowables follow NASA-STD-5001B: yield FoS 1.25, ultimate FoS 1.4. Material is typically Al 7075-T7351 or Al 6061-T6 for the primary structure. Primary verification is structural FEA (linear static for quasi-static; random-vibration harmonic response with 3-sigma stress evaluation; modal analysis for first-mode frequency). Vibration analysis is a primary gate here, not secondary. Thermal and fluid analyses are explicitly NOT required at this specific structural-qualification gate (thermal survival is verified at a separate thermal gate). Radiation analysis is explicitly NOT required since the student demo orbit is LEO. Deliverables: STEP AP242 of the primary structure, a mass budget, and a report listing wet mass in kg, first longitudinal mode in Hz, first lateral mode in Hz, max quasi-static stress in MPa with FoS, 3-sigma random-vibration peak stress in MPa, and shock-response displacement at critical mounting interfaces.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
This satellite primary structure is delivered as a multi-part bolted-panel assembly reflecting typical 50 kg-class microsatellite architecture. The speculative part decomposition is: (A) four corner rails running in the launch axis direction, (B) six side-wall panels (two per large face, plus +/-Y and +/-Z covers), (C) top and bottom deck plates, (D) internal midplate forming a payload tray, (E) launch-vehicle adapter ring at the separation plane, (F) separation-switch bracket, (G) solar-panel hinge brackets on +/-X faces, and (H) M4/M5 titanium fastener set. Inter-family interfaces: rails-to-panels bolted, panels-to-decks bolted, deck-to-midplate bolted, rails-to-adapter-ring bolted. FEM verification runs on the ASSEMBLED model only — per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Inter-part connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) at bolted joints must be ≤ 1.0 under all four load cases; adapter-ring bolts must survive LC4 pyroshock without slip. All inherited load magnitudes, material properties, and pass/fail thresholds from the original single-part brief are preserved verbatim; assembly-scope criteria are added.
short_prompt:
Design the primary structure of a 50 kg-class microsatellite for a student-demonstration microsatellite slot, delivered as a bolted-panel multi-part assembly: four corner rails (launch-axis direction) + six side-wall panels + top and bottom deck plates + internal payload-tray midplate + launch-vehicle adapter ring + separation-switch bracket + solar-panel hinge brackets + M4/M5 titanium fastener set. Envelope ~500 x 500 x 500 mm (with attachment ring); wet mass <= 50 kg. Material typically Al 7075-T7351 or Al 6061-T6. Stress allowables per NASA-STD-5001B: yield FoS 1.25, ultimate FoS 1.4.

Load cases.
- LC1 quasi-static: 20 g axial + 10 g lateral at CG.
- LC2 random vibration: 14.1 Grms NASA GEVS qualification-style 20-2000 Hz.
- LC3 pyroshock SRS: 1000 G peak in the 1000-10000 Hz band at the separation plane.
- LC4 modal: first longitudinal mode > 110 Hz, first lateral mode > 45 Hz.

Adapter-ring bolts must survive LC4 pyroshock without slip. Bolted-joint DCR <= 1.0 under all four load cases.

prompt.title: 50 kg-class microsatellite primary structure (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Design a 50 kg-class microsatellite bolted-panel primary structure comprising four corner rails, six side panels, top/bottom decks, internal payload midplate, launch-vehicle adapter ring, separation-switch bracket, solar-panel hinges, and titanium fasteners. Survives 14.1 Grms random vib, 1000 G shock SRS, 20 g axial quasi-static; first modes ≥ 110 Hz long / ≥ 45 Hz lat. FEM verification at assembled level only; connection DCR ≤ 1.0 under all governing load cases.

parts.name: corner rail
parts.role: primary launch-axis load-carrying longeron at each vertical edge of the satellite, transfers axial quasi-static load from deck to adapter ring

geometric_constraints.cross_section_mm: L or T profile, ~30 mm leg

parts.name: side-wall panel
parts.role: thin-walled panel closing lateral faces, carries shear and provides mounting surface for avionics and solar cells
parts.material_hint: Al 6061-T6 plate, optional milled pocket stiffening

parts.name: deck plate
parts.role: top and bottom closure plates carrying equipment mounts and transferring lateral loads into corner rails
parts.material_hint: Al 7075-T7351 plate with PEM or Heli-Coil inserts

parts.name: internal midplate / payload tray
parts.role: interior horizontal plate forming payload tray, stiffens structure and decouples payload from launch environment
parts.material_hint: Al 6061-T6 or honeycomb sandwich with Al facesheets

parts.name: launch-vehicle adapter ring
parts.role: interfaces bottom of spacecraft to launch vehicle payload adapter, reacts pyroshock at separation
parts.material_hint: Al 7075-T7351, machined; bolt circle per LV ICD

parts.name: separation-switch bracket
parts.role: holds deployment-switch microswitch, triggers spacecraft power-on at separation

parts.name: solar-panel hinge bracket
parts.role: mounts deployable solar-panel hinges to +/-X side panels, transfers deployed-panel inertia into primary structure

parts.name: fastener set (M4/M5)
parts.role: titanium bolts with locking features securing rails, panels, decks, midplate, and adapter ring
parts.material_hint: Ti-6Al-4V with silver-plated threads per NASA-STD-5020B, self-locking feature required

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: origin at adapter-ring center on separation plane, Z along launch axis

pass_fail_criteria.description: Bolted-joint demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) across rail-panel, panel-deck, deck-midplate, and rail-adapter interfaces ≤ 1.0 under the governing load case (typically LC1 combined with LC3 3-sigma).
No bolt preload loss > 10% after LC4 pyroshock (evaluated per NASA-STD-5020B preload-loss check on the assembled model).

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Generated coverage gate: ensures prompt-level envelope, body-count, report-field, and part geometry constraints are represented by an explicit geometry audit experiment.
Generated coverage gate: ensures the declared load case is present in the CCX deck and checked from model.dat.

interfaces.load_path: Corner rail transfers axial load into side-wall panel via bolted attachment along rail leg
Corner rail reacts deck-plate reaction at top/bottom through bolted cap
Side-wall panel transfers shear into deck plate via perimeter bolts
Deck plate supports internal midplate via threaded standoffs carrying payload-tray reactions
Corner rails bolt to launch-vehicle adapter ring, reacting axial and lateral loads through separation plane
Separation-switch bracket riveted to -Z side panel, transfers only its own small inertia
Solar-panel hinge bracket bolted to +/-X side panel, transfers deployed-panel inertia into primary structure

fem_check_policy.rationale: Microsatellite modal decoupling and 3-sigma random response depend on contact and bolted-joint stiffness between rails, panels, decks, and adapter ring; per-part FEA cannot capture the first-mode frequency that gates launch-vehicle compatibility.

notes.why_multi_part: A 50 kg-class spacecraft primary structure is inherently a bolted-panel assembly: mass optimisation and accessibility during integration preclude a monolithic design. Corner-rail architecture is the dominant approach in JAXA's Innovative Satellite Technology Demonstration selections.
notes.speculation_caveats: parts[] counts, interface bolt patterns, and dimensional ranges are inferred from public RAPIS-1/Hodoyoshi design disclosures and NASA-STD-5020B bolted-joint practice. The specific microsatellite slot may impose additional adapter-ring constraints not captured here.

generated_eval_coverage.purpose: Add runnable CCX coverage kit for JSON-only multipart prompt.
generated_eval_coverage.note: Generated checks assert solver coverage for every FEA-grade requirement, every declared load case, and prompt-level geometry constraints; they are not a domain-specific certification model.
